hero.title: Connect. Code. Innovate.
hero.subtitle: Study ICT Engineering at ITSOEH. Transform your ideas into real solutions by mastering software development, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things.

philosophy.title: More than classes: training to solve real problems.
philosophy.body: The program combines critical thinking, technical practice, and collaboration so students build engineering judgement from early stages.

formation.title: More than classes: technical training with real practice.
formation.subtitle: At ITICs, you learn by building projects, working in teams, and applying technology in real scenarios from early stages.

realAccess.title: Hands-on from day one
realAccess.body: Work with programming, networking, databases, electronics, IoT, and digital tools in academic and collaborative projects.

building.title: Official certifications
building.body: Strengthen your profile through official Huawei and Cisco academies focused on industry-recognized technology skills.

collaboration.title: Technical community
collaboration.body: Join robotics, IoT, development, and entrepreneurship clubs to keep learning beyond the classroom.

academies.title: Training aligned with industry standards.
academies.subtitle: TICs ITSOEH includes official Huawei and Cisco academies, strengthening students' technical preparation through learning pathways and industry-recognized certification opportunities.

huawei.title: Huawei ICT Academy
huawei.description: Training in information technologies, connectivity, digital infrastructure, and technological transformation.

cisco.title: Cisco Networking Academy
cisco.description: Training in networking, connectivity, switching, routing, security, and infrastructure.

certifications.title: Certifications
certifications.description: Professional evidence that complements your degree and strengthens your profile for internships, residencies, and employment.

projects.title: Projects that become portfolio evidence.
projects.subtitle: At ITICs ITSOEH, students build solutions that can be documented, published, and presented as professional evidence.

featured.name: Byron Gálvez Digital Museums
featured.description: Interactive web experiences developed for cultural outreach and digital preservation.

guides.name: ESP32 Tic-Tac-Toe
guides.description: A physical-digital project using microcontrollers, device-to-device communication, and interactive logic.

systems.name: VEX Robotics ITICs
systems.description: Participation in competitive robotics, programming, mechanical design, control, and teamwork.

infra.name: ITICs GitHub
infra.description: Institutional repository where projects, code, and technical evidence from the community are published.

mentors.title: ICT Community.
mentors.subtitle: Clubs, faculty, coordination, and students who drive your growth inside and outside the classroom.

students.title: Student leaders
students.description: Projects, workshops, community, and technical collaboration.

credits.title: Site credits
credits.description: Recognition for those who design, develop, and maintain the digital identity of TICs.

contact.title: Official contact.
contact.subtitle: Institutional channels to resolve questions about the program, admissions, curriculum, and academic activities.

programming.question: Do I need to know programming already?
programming.answer: No. The degree starts from fundamentals. What matters most is your willingness to practice, solve problems, and learn consistently.

math.question: Is there a lot of math?
math.answer: Yes, because this is an engineering degree. But it is taught progressively and applied to programming, networks, data, communications, and decision-making.

ai.question: Will AI replace engineers?
ai.answer: AI does not replace engineers who can think, design, integrate, and solve problems. AI becomes a tool for people who know how to use it and build with it.

jobs.question: Are there job opportunities?
jobs.answer: Yes. You can grow in software, networks, databases, cybersecurity, IoT, support, infrastructure, data, cloud, automation, and technology project management.

outcomes.title: What will you be able to build as an ITICs student?

items.apps: Develop web and mobile applications, plus information systems.
items.networks: Design, configure, and manage computer networks.
items.databases: Create and manage secure, reliable databases.
items.iot: Integrate devices, sensors, and IoT systems.
items.security: Apply cybersecurity and IT auditing practices.
items.entrepreneurship: Build digital solutions with real-world impact.

finalCta.title: Take the first step toward ICT Engineering.
finalCta.subtitle: Review the 2026-2027 admission process, explore the study plan, and resolve your questions with the TICs community.
